turns.note: Pipeline (SFT -> Bradley-Terry reward model -> policy optimization with KL) and all three method sketches are sound, but the deck's 'helpful, harmless, honest' framing is missing and source_mode=general — note the harness never selected 11_ppo_dpo_grpo_alignment.pptx until turn 9.

States PPO needs THREE models (policy, reference, reward) and never mentions the critic; the PA2 deck says 'needs policy, reference, reward, and critic models' — the scenario's core four-model fact is contradicted.

DPO explained via the optimal KL-regularized policy reparameterization, exact DPO loss with policy-vs-reference log-ratios for y+ and y-, reward model dropped while the reference model explicitly survives.

Says GRPO 'drops the reward model entirely — just like DPO' and needs only verifiable outcomes; the deck says GRPO drops the CRITIC and normalizes rewards within the group (the group advantage formula and +1.0 example do match Problem 9).

Headline 'all three need it' is right, but then declares GRPO's reference model 'mostly optional' / replaceable by clipping, contradicting the deck's summary that all methods use a reference model for KL control.
Comparison table lists PPO as 3 total models with no critic row anywhere and GRPO's reference as '(or optional)'; the response is also truncated mid-sentence in the loss-function table.

The numeric GRPO item exactly matches Problem 9 (mean 0.5, std 0.5, A=+1.0, clipped surrogate 1.20), but Q1 hardcodes 'PPO (3 models)' as fact and its own answer admits ambiguity ('Accept B or note that B and C tie'); answer-key summary truncated.

Student copy keeps all five questions, order and numbers, but every numeric answer is embedded in the boxed blanks as LaTeX \phantom{0.5}/\phantom{+1.0}/\phantom{1.2} — invisible when rendered yet fully readable in the distributed source.

Names the three files but attributes to the deck's slide 4 the 'policy, reference, reward, and critic' line its own turn-2 answer contradicted (the deck was only selected at this turn), and says the never-selected course outline was 'used only for context'.

highlight: PPO was taught as a 3-model method (critic omitted) and GRPO as 'drops the reward model' — both contradict the PA2 deck, which the harness never put in the selection until turn 9 — and the student quiz copy hides every numeric answer only inside LaTeX \phantom{} markup.
